title: Efficacy of Bright Light Treatment Fluoxetine and the Combination in Patients With Nonseasonal Major Depressive Disorder
notes: RCT of fluoxetine (pharma) for MDD. Canadian PI (Lam RW, UBC). Published 2016. Meets all criteria.

title: Hydromorphone Compared With Diacetylmorphine for Long-term Opioid Dependence
notes: RCT of injectable hydromorphone vs diacetylmorphine for opioid use disorder. Vancouver BC site. Published 2016. Meets all criteria.

title: Olanzapine Versus Placebo in Adult Outpatients With Anorexia Nervosa
notes: EXCLUDE: No Canadian recruitment site identified. Five North American sites listed; authors from Columbia, Johns Hopkins, Pittsburgh, U of Pittsburgh, Toronto (Kaplan AS). Kaplan is a co-author but abstract does not confirm Canadian recruitment site. Sites appear US-based. Need to verify but on abstract alone, Canadian site not confirmed. Kaplan may be co-author only without Canadian site.

title: Single repeated and maintenance ketamine infusions for treatment-resistant depression
notes: RCT of ketamine for TRD. Phillips JL, Blier P - Ottawa (Canadian). Single-site. Published 2019. Meets all criteria.

title: Efficacy of Adjunctive Infliximab vs Placebo in the Treatment of Adults With Bipolar I/II Depression
notes: RCT of infliximab for bipolar depression. Two sites: Canada and US. McIntyre RS (Toronto). Published 2019. Meets all criteria.

title: Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trial of Nabilone for Agitation in Alzheimers Disease
notes: RCT of nabilone for agitation in AD. Herrmann N, Lanctot KL - Sunnybrook Toronto. Canadian site. Published 2019. Meets all criteria.

title: Metformin for Treatment of Overweight Induced by Atypical Antipsychotic Medication in Young People With Autism Spectrum Disorder
notes: EXCLUDE: Metformin targets metabolic side effects (weight gain) NOT a mental disorder itself. The pharma intervention does not target ASD symptoms. It targets overweight/metabolic complications. Fails criterion 3.

title: Single and repeated ketamine infusions for reduction of suicidal ideation in treatment-resistant depression
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary analysis of PMID 30922101. Explicitly states "The purpose of this secondary analysis was to evaluate reduction in SI." Same trial, different outcome. Fails exclusion criterion for secondary/post-hoc analyses.

title: Effect of Methylphenidate on Apathy in Patients With Alzheimer Disease: The ADMET 2 Randomized Clinical Trial
notes: RCT of methylphenidate for apathy in AD. 9 US + 1 Canadian clinic. Lanctot KL (Toronto), Herrmann N. Published 2021. Meets all criteria.

title: Effect of Continuing Olanzapine vs Placebo on Relapse Among Patients With Psychotic Depression in Remission: The STOP-PD II
notes: RCT of olanzapine continuation for psychotic depression. 4 academic centers. Flint AJ (Toronto). Published 2019. Canadian PI. Meets all criteria.

title: Effectiveness of Sequential Psychological and Medication Therapies for Insomnia Disorder
notes: RCT of sequential BT and zolpidem for insomnia disorder. Morin CM - Universite Laval Quebec. Canadian site. Published 2020. Pharma arm (zolpidem). Meets all criteria.

title: Treating Insulin Resistance With Metformin as a Strategy to Improve Clinical Outcomes in Treatment-Resistant Bipolar Depression (TRIO-BD)
notes: RCT of metformin to reverse insulin resistance and improve bipolar depression outcomes. Calkin CV, Alda M - Dalhousie, Halifax. 2-site Canadian. Published 2022. Pharma targets depression improvement. Meets all criteria.

title: Exploratory Tau Biomarker Results From a Multiple Ascending-Dose Study of BIIB080 in Alzheimer Disease
notes: Phase 1b RCT of BIIB080 (tau-targeting ASO) in mild AD. 12 sites in Canada and Europe. Published 2023. Primary trial results (biomarker endpoints). Meets all criteria.

title: Agomelatine or placebo as adjunctive therapy to a mood stabiliser in bipolar I depression
notes: EXCLUDE: International multisite trial. Yatham LN is listed but abstract does not specify Canadian recruitment sites. Authors include Vieta E (Spain), Goodwin GM (UK), Bourin M (France), Calabrese J (US). Trial registration ISRCTN (non-US registry). No evidence of Canadian site from abstract. Likely non-Canadian recruitment.

title: Heterogeneity of treatment response to citalopram for patients with Alzheimers disease with aggression or agitation: The CitAD randomized clinical trial
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary analysis. Assesses "heterogeneity of response" using "post-hoc exploratory analysis" methods including "two-stage multivariate method" on previously published CitAD trial data. Not main trial results.

title: Lurasidone versus treatment as usual for cognitive impairment in euthymic patients with bipolar I disorder
notes: RCT of lurasidone for cognitive impairment in bipolar I. Yatham LN - UBC Hospital Vancouver. Canadian site. Published 2017. Pharma targets bipolar-related cognitive impairment. Meets all criteria.

title: Efficacy of adjunctive infliximab vs placebo in the treatment of anhedonia in bipolar I/II depression
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary analysis of the infliximab bipolar trial (NCT02363738, same as PMID 31066887). States "primary outcome measure for the present secondary analysis was change in the SHAPS total score." Not main trial results.

title: Psilocybin-assisted psychotherapy for treatment resistant depression
notes: RCT of psilocybin-assisted psychotherapy for TRD. Rosenblat JD - Toronto. Canadian site (NCT05029466). Published 2024. Meets all criteria.

title: Feasibility and safety of extended-release naltrexone treatment of opioid and alcohol use disorder in HIV clinics
notes: RCT of XR-naltrexone for OUD/AUD in HIV clinics. Vancouver BC site (Ahamad K, Wood E). Published 2017. Canadian recruitment. Meets all criteria.

title: Extracellular Vesicle Biomarkers Reveal Inhibition of Neuroinflammation by Infliximab in Association with Antidepressant Response in Adults with Bipolar Depression
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary/mechanistic analysis of the infliximab bipolar trial (NCT02363738, same as PMID 31066887). Biomarker analysis from the same trial. Not main trial results.

title: Micronutrients for ADHD in Youths (MADDY)
notes: EXCLUDE: Micronutrients (vitamins/minerals) are NOT pharmacological intervention. This is a nutraceutical/supplement study. Three US sites (Ohio State, Oregon Health Sciences, Lethbridge-like). Authors Johnstone, Hatsu, Arnold (US). Leung BMY may be Canadian but no Canadian site confirmed. Also borderline non-pharma. Fails criterion 3 (pharma).

title: Efficacy of Adjunctive D-Cycloserine to Intermittent Theta-Burst Stimulation for Major Depressive Disorder
notes: RCT of D-cycloserine adjunctive to iTBS for MDD. McGirr A - University of Calgary. Single Canadian site. Published 2022. D-cycloserine is pharmacological. Meets all criteria.

title: Methylene blue treatment for residual symptoms of bipolar disorder: randomised crossover study
notes: RCT (crossover) of methylene blue for bipolar disorder. Alda M - Dalhousie. Canadian. Published 2017. Meets all criteria.

title: Optimal duration of risperidone or olanzapine adjunctive therapy to mood stabilizer following remission of a manic episode: A CANMAT randomized double-blind trial
notes: RCT of antipsychotic duration for bipolar I. CANMAT trial - multiple Canadian sites. Yatham LN. Published 2016. Meets all criteria.

title: A randomized double-blind placebo-controlled crossover trial evaluating the effect of intranasal insulin on cognition and mood in individuals with treatment-resistant MDD
notes: RCT of intranasal insulin for cognition/mood in TRD. McIntyre RS, Kennedy SH - Toronto. Canadian. Published 2017. Meets all criteria.

title: Brain serotonin synthesis capacity in obsessive-compulsive disorder: effects of cognitive behavioral therapy and sertraline
notes: RCT of CBT vs sertraline for OCD with PET neuroimaging. Benkelfat C - Montreal. Canadian site. Published 2018. Pharma arm (sertraline). Meets all criteria.

title: Optimizing Outcomes of Treatment-Resistant Depression in Older Adults (OPTIMUM)
notes: EXCLUDE: This is a study design/protocol paper. Title includes "Study Design and Treatment Characteristics of the First 396 Participants Randomized." Reports methodology and baseline characteristics, NOT main trial results. States "To date, 396 participants have been randomized." Protocol/interim report only.

title: The association between lithium use and neurocognitive performance in patients with bipolar disorder
notes: EXCLUDE: Not main trial results. This is a secondary analysis of a multi-site ongoing clinical trial of lithium monotherapy. Cross-sectional comparison of lithium users vs non-users at baseline, then longitudinal subset analysis. Not a primary RCT report.

title: Norepinephrine transporter gene variants and remission from depression with venlafaxine treatment in older adults
notes: EXCLUDE: Pharmacogenomic secondary analysis. Investigates gene variants predicting remission in a three-site clinical trial of venlafaxine. Not the main trial results paper. Marshe VS (Toronto) but this is a secondary genetics analysis.

title: The network structure of core depressive symptom-domains in MDD following antidepressant treatment: a randomized clinical trial
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary network analysis. Uses data from a randomized trial of escitalopram vs desvenlafaxine to perform network analysis of symptom domains. This is a secondary analytical approach, not the primary trial results. Berlim MT, Turecki G - Montreal.

title: Evaluation of BI 409306 a novel phosphodiesterase type 9 inhibitor in two phase II studies in prodromal and mild Alzheimers disease
notes: EXCLUDE: No Canadian involvement identified. Authors are Frolich L (Germany), Wunderlich G, Thamer C, Roehrle M, Garcia M (Boehringer Ingelheim), Dubois B (France). Sites in North America and Europe but no Canadian authors or sites evident.

title: A Randomized Placebo-Controlled Discontinuation Study of Cholinesterase Inhibitors in Institutionalized Patients With Moderate to Severe Alzheimer Disease
notes: RCT of ChEI discontinuation in AD. Herrmann N, Lanctot KL - Sunnybrook Toronto. Canadian. Published 2016. Meets all criteria.

title: Factors associated with relapse in schizophrenia despite adherence to long-acting injectable antipsychotic therapy
notes: EXCLUDE: Post-hoc exploratory analysis. Explicitly states "This was a post-hoc exploratory analysis of a 1-year study." Secondary analysis of NCT00297388. Not main trial results. Also, Alphs L and authors are Janssen (pharma industry). Canada mentioned only as a variable in the regression.

title: Impact of Reference and Target Region Selection on Amyloid PET SUV Ratios in the Phase 1b PRIME Study of Aducanumab
notes: EXCLUDE: Methodological/imaging analysis of the PRIME study. Explores impact of ROI selection on PET SUV ratios. Not main trial results. Chiao P, Bedell BJ - Biospective (Montreal company) but this is a secondary imaging methodology paper.

title: Donepezil for gait and falls in mild cognitive impairment: a randomized controlled trial
notes: RCT of donepezil for gait/falls in MCI. Montero-Odasso M - Western University London ON, Chertkow H, Borrie M. Canadian sites. Published 2019. Meets all criteria.

title: Exploring brain insulin resistance in adults with bipolar depression using extracellular vesicles of neuronal origin
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary/mechanistic analysis from the infliximab bipolar trial (NCT02363738, same parent trial as PMID 31066887). Biomarker analysis exploring neuronal insulin signaling. Not main trial results.

title: Oral Tau Aggregation Inhibitor for Alzheimers Disease: Design Progress and Basis for Selection of the 16 mg/day Dose
notes: EXCLUDE: Study design/protocol paper for the LUCIDITY trial. Describes rationale and design, reports "446 participants are expected to complete." Gauthier S (Canadian) is an author. No main trial results reported yet. Protocol without results.

title: A Randomized Placebo-Controlled Trial of Metformin for the Treatment of Overweight Induced by Antipsychotic Medication in Young People With ASD: Open-Label Extension
notes: EXCLUDE: (1) Open-label extension, not main RCT results (main RCT is PMID 27556593). (2) Metformin targets weight gain, not a mental disorder. Fails on both secondary analysis and criterion 3.

title: Vortioxetine improves symptomatic and functional outcomes in major depressive disorder
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary/post-hoc analysis. Describes "This analysis evaluated the combined effect of vortioxetine" using data from NCT01564862 - a multinational trial. Authors Christensen MC, Loft H (Lundbeck Denmark), McIntyre RS. Post-hoc dual outcome analysis of existing trial data. Not main results.

title: A randomized comparison of long acting methylphenidate and cognitive behavioral therapy in the treatment of binge eating disorder
notes: RCT of methylphenidate vs CBT for BED. Quilty LC, Kaplan AS - Toronto (CAMH). Canadian. Published 2019. Pharma arm (methylphenidate). Meets all criteria.

title: Comorbid anxiety in late-life depression: Relationship with remission and suicidal ideation on venlafaxine treatment
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary analysis. Examines "influence of comorbid anxiety symptoms on antidepressant treatment remission" in a multisite clinical trial. Uses logistic regression/survival analysis on existing trial data. Mulsant BH (Toronto) is co-author but this is not the main trial results paper. Post-hoc analysis.

title: Peripheral inflammatory biomarkers define biotypes of bipolar depression
notes: EXCLUDE: Secondary analysis of the infliximab bipolar trial (NCT02363738, same parent trial as PMID 31066887). Machine learning-based biomarker analysis to identify inflammatory biotypes. Not main trial results.
